delta.comment: External dependency SATISFIED. The released backlogit hierarchical-sizing contract is available and was verified at runtime against backlogit v1.6.0-356 in a backlogit-rooted session (backlogit repo added to workspace). Authoritative contract: backlogit docs/design-docs/2026-07-19-size-estimation-contract.md (feature 108-F / shipment 099-S). Requirement-by-requirement verification: (1) level-relative ordinal size enum XS<S<M<L<XL, no numeric points, no runtime comparator; (2) provenance completeness enforced (size_source in human|agent|derived requires non-empty size_ruleset_version; MCP/agent transport cannot claim human); (3) re-estimation history via fail-closed event-before-write estimate_history events on the item log that preserve the planned estimate captured before work ran, with the durable custom_fields.size as source of truth; (4) stable CLI/read behavior with SIZE/COMPOSITION columns and size_composition on get/list JSON at CLI/MCP parity; (5) stable tasks-only hierarchy/membership via computed-on-read size_composition. RECONCILIATION (task-only storage): size is stored ONLY on task artifacts. Features and shipments never store a size label/provenance; they expose a computed-on-read size_composition (XS-XL histogram, unsized count, de-duplicated tasks-only canonical members, skipped list; ruleset_version always null). The WorkSizingSnapshot adapter must therefore record feature/shipment stored size labels and their per-level source/revision/ruleset as null-by-contract (not missing-data), snapshot the composition count/histogram, and DERIVE the membership hash host-side (ratified lowercase SHA-256 hex over the UTF-8 JSON array of unique task IDs sorted bytewise) from the canonical members array, since backlogit returns members but no hash. CAVEATS the adapter must honor: durability is process-crash-safe-only (no OS-crash reconciliation, exactly-once, or doctor reconciliation); size_composition is a best-effort non-atomic multi-read view, so the pre-execution capture must sync the index first for strict freshness, then capture-once-and-freeze to preserve epoch immutability. This resolves the blocked-on-external condition; label removed and task moved to queued.
